pick_notes.3:
A longer-priced flag to open the Del Mar card. We give Zip Me Up about a 19% chance in the five-furlong turf dash — not the likeliest winner we'll name today, but the board has him out past where our number sits, and that gap is where a payout actually lives. He's our top figure in a full sprint, four of them gunning early.

Fair is 9/2; the price to demand is 5/1 or better, and we'd skip it underneath. In a five-eighths turf blast the trip matters as much as the figure — one bad step from the gate and the race is gone — so this is a swing, not a stand. One unit at the number, no regret if the speed sorts out against him.
pick_notes.6:
The shape here is what stands out. It's a maiden dirt sprint, and nobody in it has shown reliable early speed — no confirmed front-runner in the field. In a five-furlong dash that's a real edge for a horse who can break and go, because whoever grabs the lead in a race with no natural speed often gets to set it cheaply. Net Par is our top figure at 28%, and this is the kind of setup that rewards the best of a soft-pace group.

Fair 5/2, take 3/1 or better. Maidens are maidens — a first-time starter can wake up and green trips happen, and there's no long form to lean on. But the pace picture is doing our pick a favor, and at a fair price that's worth the play.
pick_notes.7:
A route on the Del Mar turf, and the pace reads clean. Only one horse in here really wants the front, so we're not expecting a duel — which usually means the race gets run on the leader's terms instead of blowing apart early. Centrodelantero is our top figure at 22%, a step clear of what the board is offering.

Fair is 7/2, play 4/1 or better. A mile-and-a-sixteenth on grass off a light profile means class and finish decide it, not raw speed, and that's the part our figures like. Moderate edge, honest price — a straightforward unit if the number's there.
pick_notes.11:
We close Del Mar on our second-biggest overlay of the day. Koekkoek is a maiden going a flat mile on the turf, and we make him a 27% chance where the board has him more than 3/1 longer than that — nearly eleven points of edge, behind only our Saratoga route flag. Top figure, even pace, a trip that lets a genuine horse settle and finish.

Fair 5/2, and take 3/1 or better. The honest caveat: it's a maiden route, so the form is thin and one bad beginning can end it before the stretch. But an eleven-point gap gives you room to be a little wrong and still come out ahead. A unit at the price, and let the closing number be the closing number.

subject: DMR · Sat Jul 18 — 11 races, 4 flags, 7 passes
